titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Lonsdale to Sir Matthew Nathan, 28 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Lonsdale

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Robert Lonsdale, Crown Solicitor of Leitrim, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland, offering his candidacy for the position of Chief Crown Solicitor, following the death of Malachy J. Kelly.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5760/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Manorhamilton,
Co Leitrim. March 28th 1916 Sir. A vacancy having arisen in the
office of Chief Crown Solicitor.
I beg to tender myself as an
applicant for the vacancy.
I have been resident & practising
as a Solicitor in the County of Leitrim
for the last 26 years and for over 15 of
which I have discharged the duties
of the office of Crown & Sessional Crown
Solicitor for that County. If I am
appointed I shall endeavour to give
every satisfaction in the discharge
of the duties of the office. I am Sir
Your Obedient Servant Rob Lonsdale To
The Under Secretary
Chief Secretary Office
